regions: Pacific slope of western South America
nativeRange.habitat: Coastal streams and rivers of the Pacific slope, generally over sandy substrate with rock and woody cover.
nativeRange.biotope: South American coastal river / stream biotope with sandy substrate, driftwood, and scattered rockwork.

decorPreferences: avoid delicate rooted plants (they tend to dig)

diet.notes: Generally accepts a varied diet of sinking cichlid pellets, frozen and live foods (earthworms, prawns, mussel, krill), and occasional vegetable matter (blanched peas, spinach). Have been reported to do best on a moderate-protein diet; very fatty mammalian meats are commonly advised against.

goodWith: other large, robust South/Central American cichlids in suitably sized tanks
avoidWith: Corydoras and other small bottom dwellers
African rift-lake cichlids (very different water and behaviour)

summary: Large, boldly coloured South American cichlid from Pacific-slope drainages of Ecuador and Peru. Generally considered an intermediate-level fish that is highly territorial and best kept singly or as a bonded pair in a long tank.
careNotes:
Andinoacara rivulatus is the species commonly traded as the 'Gold Saum' Green Terror, distinguished by an orange-to-gold edge on the dorsal and caudal fins. The very similar 'Silver Saum' Green Terror, historically lumped under this name, is now recognised as a separate species, Andinoacara stalsbergi, with a white fin margin and a more restricted Peruvian range. Most fish sold in the trade as 'Green Terror' are A. rivulatus (Gold Saum); buyers wanting the true Silver Saum should confirm species ID with the seller.

Sources disagree fairly widely on minimum tank size: SeriouslyFish recommends a ~150 cm / ~650 L tank when kept with compatible tankmates, while hobby sources like Fishkeeping World quote 35 US gal (~132 L) for a single fish and 75 US gal (~284 L) for a pair. A 300 L / 150 cm-long tank is used here as a conservative-but-realistic single-fish minimum; a bonded pair or community of large cichlids realistically wants 450–650 L or more. This spread is the reason dataStatus is 'needs_review' — a human reviewer should sanity-check this figure against current best practice.

Reported adult size also varies between sources (FishBase max 20 cm TL, hobby sources 20–25 cm in aquaria, SeriouslyFish 22–30 cm SL); 20–30 cm is used here as the spanning range. Conspecific aggression is consistently described as very high — untested males commonly kill females if introduced directly, so a common approach is to raise a group of 6+ juveniles and let a pair form naturally, then remove the others.

Primary source used was SeriouslyFish (Aquarium Co-Op, the configured slice primary, has no dedicated Green Terror care page — its search returned only merchandise and an unrelated terrarium-plant article). FishBase and Fishkeeping World were used as additional cross-checks. Practical Fishkeeping returned HTTP 403 during research and could not be consulted. [2026-06-12 review]: tank floor set to 300 L baseline for a single pair (matches Fishkeeping World pair guideline and a one-step-larger margin for the species territoriality). Specialist-source 650+ L recommendations apply once tankmates are present.
breedingNotes: Has been reported to breed readily in captivity once a compatible pair has formed. Pairs typically clean a flat rock or smooth surface and deposit up to several hundred eggs (FishBase notes up to ~600). Both parents tend the eggs and fry, with the female fanning and the male defending the territory; eggs generally hatch in 3–4 days and fry are free-swimming around 6–8 days. Breeding pairs become extremely aggressive toward other tank inhabitants and may kill tankmates, so a dedicated breeding tank is commonly advised.

primary.name: SeriouslyFish
primary.notes: Swapped to primary because the configured slice primary (Aquarium Co-Op) has no Green Terror care page — its on-site search returned only merchandise/unrelated articles, and direct guesses at /blogs/aquarium/green-terror-cichlid and /products/green-terror both returned 404.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Used for authoritative taxonomy (Cichlidae / Cichliformes), distribution, IUCN status (Least Concern, 2020), max length (20 cm TL), and breeding biology (up to ~600 eggs, biparental care).

additional.name: Fishkeeping World
additional.notes: Hobby cross-check on adult size (~20 cm aquarium / ~30 cm wild), lifespan (7–10 years), care level (intermediate), and the Gold Saum vs Silver Saum (A. stalsbergi) trade distinction. Quoted minimum tank size of 35 US gal single / 75 US gal pair is markedly smaller than SeriouslyFish's ~650 L figure.

additional.name: Practical Fishkeeping
additional.notes: Attempted as a fourth cross-check; site returned HTTP 403 Forbidden and could not be consulted.

fish.breedingNotes: Pairs spawn on flat surfaces; biparental care of eggs and fry. Compatible pair-formation is the main challenge — best obtained by raising 6+ juveniles and removing the non-pair once a pair establishes.
